name: Rice-Stuffed Artichokes
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the stems and loose outer leaves from the artichokes. Cut off about an inch from the tops.
2. Snip off the sharp leaf tips. Brush the cut edges with 2 teaspoons lemon juice.
3. Place the artichokes and water in a casserole dish. Cover with vented clear plastic wrap.
4. Cook on high power for 7 to 9 minutes, or until tender. Rotate the casserole half-turn after 4 minutes.
5. Let stand covered while preparing the stuffing.
6. For the stuffing, stir together shredded carrot, sliced green onion, butter or margarine, and crushed sage in a small non-metal bowl.
7. Cook on high power for 2½ to 3½ minutes, stirring once, until vegetables are tender.
8. Stir together the vegetable mixture and cooked rice.
9. Drain the artichokes. Remove the center leaves and choke from each artichoke.
10. Spread the remaining leaf slightly. Spoon the rice stuffing into the center of each artichoke and behind each large leaf.
11. Return the artichokes to the casserole dish. Cover with vented clear plastic wrap.
12. Cook on high power for 2 to 3 minutes, or until hot and the bases of the artichokes are fork-tender. Rotate the casserole a half-turn every minute.
13. Let stand covered while preparing the sauce.
14. For the sauce, stir together chicken broth, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, cornstarch, and white pepper in a 2-cup measure.
15. Cook on high power for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring every 30 seconds, until thickened and bubbly.
16. Stir half of the hot sauce into the egg yolk.
17. Return all to the measuring cup and cook uncovered for 30 seconds more.
18. Transfer stuffed artichokes to a warm serving platter. Pour the sauce around the artichokes.
